content.verdict: Cocobay is a hillside collection of pastel-painted wooden cottages tumbling down to two small beaches on Antigua's western coast — adults-only, all-inclusive, and known for one of the highest couples-percentages in the Caribbean. The cottages are spread across a steep hill, each with a private terrace and panoramic ocean views, creating one of the most romantic and unique honeymoon settings on the island.
content.best_room: The Premium Plunge Pool Cottages have private plunge pools on the terrace and hill positions with the best views. The Waterfront Cottages on the lower slopes are closest to the beach. The Honeymoon Cottage at the top of the property is a dedicated category with the most expansive terrace and views. For honeymoons specifically, Premium Plunge Pool Cottages are the sweet spot.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Cottage Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from V.C. Bird International (35 minutes to Valley Church Bay on the western coast). Cocobay's all-inclusive includes premium spirits, wines, and water sports. Welcome drink at the hilltop bar, then settle into your cottage with the panoramic Caribbean view from the terrace. First dinner at Hummingbird, the main restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach Day & Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Cocobay's two small beaches are quiet and swimmer-friendly. Morning paddleboard and snorkel along the rocky headland. Afternoon at the open-air spa pavilion (set on the hillside with ocean views) for a couples' treatment, then sunset cocktails on the cottage terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nelson's Dockyard & English Harbour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive south across the island (40 minutes) to Nelson's Dockyard, the UNESCO World Heritage Georgian naval dockyard where Admiral Nelson commanded the British fleet in the 1780s. Lunch at the harbourside, walk through the restored 18th-century buildings, and afternoon at Pigeon Point Beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Shirley Heights Sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: A drive up to Shirley Heights, the 18th-century military lookout above English Harbour, for the iconic sunset view down to the yachts. Sunday evenings bring the steel band gathering with barbecue and rum punch — Antigua's most famous social ritual.

itinerary_7_nights.title: 365 Beaches Tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Antigua's 365 beaches — one for every day of the year — invite a day of beach-hopping. A private driver day visits Half Moon Bay (Atlantic side, surf), Pink Sand Beach, Ffryes Bay (next door to Cocobay, beach bars), and Darkwood Beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Stingray City & Sailing
itinerary_7_nights.description: Boat trip to Stingray City off Antigua's north coast — a shallow sandbar where southern stingrays glide in waist-deep water. Combine with a snorkel stop at offshore reefs. Afternoon at Cocobay for kayaking around the small headland.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Cottage Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last swim at the bay, breakfast on the cottage terrace with the panoramic view, and a 35-minute road transfer back to V.C. Bird International. Most flights depart in the afternoon, allowing a slow final morning at the cottage.

caveats: Cocobay is built on a steep hill — daily walking up and down between cottage and beach is significant; couples with mobility issues will struggle. Buggies are available on request but limited.
The cottages, while charming, are simple wooden structures — couples expecting full luxury hotel finishes will find them rustic. The romance is in the views and atmosphere, not the materials.
The two beaches are small and can feel busy at peak occupancy — neighbouring Ffryes Bay is a 5-minute walk and offers more space.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Cocobay is a textbook honeymoon hotel — adults-only, all-inclusive, with a stunning hillside cottage layout and one of the Caribbean's highest couples-review percentages. The terrace views, the sunsets, and the romantic intimacy of the wooden cottages create an unbeatable honeymoon atmosphere at moderate prices.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April is high season — dry and sunny. February-March is peak. May, June, and November offer lower rates. September-October is hurricane season; the resort sometimes closes for refurbishment.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: For Christmas, New Year, and February-March, book 6-9 months ahead. Premium Plunge Pool Cottages and the Honeymoon Cottage sell out first. Shoulder season offers good availability with 2-4 months lead time.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Yes — Cocobay is strictly adults-only (18+). The hillside layout, intimate scale, and couples-focused programming make it one of Antigua's most consistently romantic adults-only properties.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Premium Plunge Pool Cottages for the combination of view, terrace, and private plunge pool. The Honeymoon Cottage at the top of the property has the largest terrace and most panoramic views. Waterfront Cottages on the lower slopes trade height for proximity to the beach.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to V.C. Bird International Airport (ANU). Cocobay is 35 minutes by road on the western coast at Valley Church — the resort arranges private transfers. Antigua receives direct flights from London, New York, Miami, Atlanta, Toronto, and major European hubs.
